whatItDoes: Expands a CIDR range into a complete list of individual IP addresses. Enter any CIDR notation (e.g., 192.168.1.0/30) and see every address in the range with a click-to-copy grid. Includes a warning for large ranges and a bulk copy button.
whyItExists: When provisioning services, whitelisting IPs, or debugging network filters, engineers often need to enumerate every address in a CIDR block. Manually listing them is impractical for ranges larger than a handful of hosts.
whoShouldUse: Network administrators configuring firewall rules and ACLs, DevOps engineers provisioning cloud resources, and security analysts reviewing IP allow-lists.
useCases: Enumerate all IPs in a /28 subnet to plan static IP assignments for a server cluster
Generate a complete IP list from a CIDR block to populate a firewall allow-list
Quickly see how many usable addresses exist in a given subnet for capacity planning
Copy a list of IPs from a small range to paste into a monitoring tool configuration
instructions: Enter a CIDR notation (e.g., 192.168.1.0/24) in the input field
Press Expand or hit Enter to list all addresses in the range
Click any individual IP to copy it to your clipboard
Use the Copy All button to copy the entire list at once
examples: Input: '10.0.0.0/29' — Output: 10.0.0.0 through 10.0.0.7 (8 total, 6 usable hosts)
bestPractices: Use smaller prefix lengths (/28 to /30) for manual enumeration; larger ranges are better handled programmatically
Remember that the first and last addresses in a subnet are typically the network and broadcast addresses
Copy the full list when generating firewall rules to avoid missing addresses
commonMistakes: Entering a prefix like /16 which produces 65,536 addresses — use a larger CIDR for manageable lists
Forgetting that the network and broadcast addresses may not be assignable to hosts
Not checking the warning for large ranges before expanding
faq: What is the maximum range size? — The tool supports up to /16 (65,536 addresses) but will warn for ranges over 1,024 IPs.
Does it show network and broadcast addresses? — Yes, all addresses including network and broadcast are listed.
Can I export the list? — Use the Copy All button to copy the full list to your clipboard for pasting into a file or configuration.
